turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Hi doctor. I’ve had a dry cough and I’ve been a bit tired for about two weeks. I’m also here for my blood pressure follow-up.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Good morning. Let’s go through both issues. Have you had any fever, shortness of breath, chest pain, or coughing up phlegm or blood?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No fever. No chest pain. I’m not short of breath. It’s mostly just a dry cough, especially when I talk a lot at work.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Okay. Any sore throat, runny nose, wheezing, heartburn, or post-nasal drip? And have you had any sick contacts recently?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: I do get a bit of a tickle in my throat. I’ve had some sneezing too, but that happens with my allergies. No heartburn. I don’t know about sick contacts.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: That helps. Is the cough worse at night, when you lie down, or after exercise? And have you noticed any weight loss, night sweats, or feeling very unwell?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, none of that. It’s not really worse at night. Mostly during the day when I’m speaking a lot. I haven’t lost weight.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Good. Tell me about your blood pressure medication. Are you taking the amlodipine every day? Any side effects like ankle swelling, dizziness, or headaches?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Yes, I take it daily. I only miss it now and then if I’m rushing. No dizziness. My ankles maybe swell a little by the evening, but not badly.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Thank you. Have you checked your blood pressure at home or elsewhere recently?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: I checked at a pharmacy last week. It was around 134 over 84. I can’t remember the exact number, but it was not too high.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: That sounds fairly reasonable. I’d like to ask a few more general questions. Do you smoke or vape, and do you have any asthma or lung problems?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, I don’t smoke or vape. And I’ve never had asthma. I do have seasonal allergies, though.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Understood. Have you taken anything for the cough besides your cetirizine? Any new medicines, including blood pressure tablets or over-the-counter remedies?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, just the cetirizine sometimes. No new medicines.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Thanks. On examination your blood pressure is a little above target today, but not dangerously high. Your chest sounds clear, and I’m not finding anything worrying. This cough sounds most like irritation from allergies or throat tickle, possibly made worse by talking a lot at work.

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Okay. So it’s probably not anything serious?

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: At this stage, it does not sound serious. I’d suggest good fluid intake, avoiding throat irritants, and using your cetirizine more regularly for a short period if your allergies are active. For the blood pressure, keep taking the amlodipine daily and try not to miss doses. We can also review whether the ankle swelling is from the medication if it becomes more noticeable.

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Alright. Should I come back if the cough stays on?

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Yes. If the cough is not improving over the next two to three weeks, or if you develop fever, shortness of breath, chest pain, blood in the sputum, wheezing, or unexplained weight loss, please come back sooner. Otherwise, I’d like you to book a routine follow-up for your blood pressure and cough review, and we can consider further tests if it persists.
